editor.label: Allow Cheats
editor.description:
• If true then cheats like commands can be used.
  • Allowed values: "true" or "false"

editor.label: Compression Threshold
editor.description: • Determines the smallest size of raw network payload to compress

editor.label: Enable Content Logging
editor.description: • Enables logging content errors to a file

editor.label: Correct Player Movement
editor.description: • If true, the client position will get corrected to the server position if the movement score exceeds the threshold.

editor.label: Default Player Permission Level
editor.description:
• The permission level for new players joining for the first time.
  • Allowed values: "visitor", "member", "operator"

editor.label: Difficulty
editor.description:
• Sets the difficulty of the world.
  • Allowed values: "peaceful", "easy", "normal", or "hard"

editor.label: Force Game Mode
editor.description:
• force-gamemode=false (or force-gamemode is not defined in the server.properties)
  • prevents the server from sending to the client gamemode values other
  • than the gamemode value saved by the server during world creation
  • even if those values are set in server.properties after world creation.
  • force-gamemode=true forces the server to send to the client gamemode values
  • other than the gamemode value saved by the server during world creation
  • if those values are set in server.properties after world creation.

editor.label: Game Mode
editor.description:
• Sets the game mode for new players.
  • Allowed values: "survival", "creative", or "adventure"

editor.label: World Name
editor.description: • Allowed values: Any string without semicolon symbol or symbols illegal for file name: /\n\r\t\f`?*\\<>|\":

editor.label: World Seed
editor.description:
• Use to randomize the world
  • Allowed values: Any string

editor.label: Max Player Slots
editor.description:
• The maximum number of players that can play on the server.
  • Allowed values: Any positive integer

editor.label: Max threads
editor.description:
• Maximum number of threads the server will try to use. If set to 0 or removed then it will use as many as possible.
  • Allowed values: Any positive integer.

editor.label: Online Mode
editor.description:
• If true then all connected players must be authenticated to Xbox Live.
  • Clients connecting to remote (non-LAN) servers will always require Xbox Live authentication regardless of this setting.
  • If the server accepts connections from the Internet, then it's highly recommended to enable online-mode.
  • Allowed values: "true" or "false"

editor.label: Player Idle Timeout
editor.description:
• After a player has idled for this many minutes they will be kicked. If set to 0 then players can idle indefinitely.
  • Allowed values: Any non-negative integer.

editor.label: Player Movement Distance Threshold
editor.description:
• The difference between server and client positions that needs to be exceeded before abnormal behavior is detected.
  • Disabled by server-authoritative-movement.

editor.label: Player Movement Duration Threshold in milliseconds
editor.description:
• The duration of time the server and client positions can be out of sync (as defined by player-movement-distance-threshold)
  • before the abnormal movement score is incremented. This value is defined in milliseconds.
  • Disabled by server-authoritative-movement.

editor.label: Player Movement Score threshold
editor.description:
• The number of incongruent time intervals needed before abnormal behavior is reported.
  • Disabled by server-authoritative-movement.

editor.label: Server Authoritative Block Breaking
editor.description: • If true, the server will compute block mining operations in sync with the client so it can verify that the client should be able to break blocks when it thinks it can.

editor.label: Server Authoritative Movement
editor.description:
• Enables server authoritative movement. If "server-auth", the server will replay local user input on
  • the server and send down corrections when the client's position doesn't match the server's.
  • If "server-auth-with-rewind" is enabled and the server sends a correction, the clients will be instructed
  • to rewind time back to the correction time, apply the correction, then replay all the player's inputs since then. This results in smoother and more frequent corrections.
  • Corrections will only happen if correct-player-movement is set to true.

editor.label: Texturepack Required
editor.description: • Force clients to use texture packs in the current world

editor.label: Tick Distance
editor.description:
• The world will be ticked this many chunks away from any player.
  • Allowed values: Integers in the range [4, 12]

editor.label: View Distance
editor.description:
• The maximum allowed view distance in a number of chunks.
  • Allowed values: Positive integer equal to 5 or greater.

editor.label: White List
editor.description:
• If true then all connected players must be listed in the separate whitelist.json file.
  • Allowed values: "true" or "false"

configFiles.template:
server-name=${Hostname}
# Used as the server name
# Allowed values: Any string without semicolon symbol.

gamemode=${gamemode}
# Sets the game mode for new players.
# Allowed values: "survival", "creative", or "adventure"

force-gamemode=${forceGamemode}
# force-gamemode=false (or force-gamemode  is not defined in the server.properties)
# prevents the server from sending to the client gamemode values other
# than the gamemode value saved by the server during world creation
# even if those values are set in server.properties after world creation.
# 
# force-gamemode=true forces the server to send to the client gamemode values
# other than the gamemode value saved by the server during world creation
# if those values are set in server.properties after world creation.

difficulty=${difficulty}
# Sets the difficulty of the world.
# Allowed values: "peaceful", "easy", "normal", or "hard"

allow-cheats=${allowCheats}
# If true then cheats like commands can be used.
# Allowed values: "true" or "false"

max-players=${maxPlayers}
# The maximum number of players that can play on the server.
# Allowed values: Any positive integer

online-mode=${onlineMode}
# If true then all connected players must be authenticated to Xbox Live.
# Clients connecting to remote (non-LAN) servers will always require Xbox Live authentication regardless of this setting.
# If the server accepts connections from the Internet, then it's highly recommended to enable online-mode.
# Allowed values: "true" or "false"

white-list=${whiteList}
# If true then all connected players must be listed in the separate whitelist.json file.
# Allowed values: "true" or "false"

server-port=${GamePort}
# Which IPv4 port the server should listen to.
# Allowed values: Integers in the range [1, 65535]

server-portv6=${CustomPort1}
# Which IPv6 port the server should listen to.
# Allowed values: Integers in the range [1, 65535]

view-distance=${viewDistance}
# The maximum allowed view distance in number of chunks.
# Allowed values: Positive integer equal to 5 or greater.

tick-distance=${tickDistance}
# The world will be ticked this many chunks away from any player.
# Allowed values: Integers in the range [4, 12]

player-idle-timeout=${playerIdleTimeout}
# After a player has idled for this many minutes they will be kicked. If set to 0 then players can idle indefinitely.
# Allowed values: Any non-negative integer.

max-threads=${maxThreads}
# Maximum number of threads the server will try to use. If set to 0 or removed then it will use as many as possible.
# Allowed values: Any positive integer.

level-name=${levelName}
# Allowed values: Any string without semicolon symbol or symbols illegal for file name: /\n\r\t\f`?*\\<>|\":

level-seed=${levelSeed}
# Use to randomize the world
# Allowed values: Any string

default-player-permission-level=${defaultPlayerPermissionLevel}
# Permission level for new players joining for the first time.
# Allowed values: "visitor", "member", "operator"

texturepack-required=${texturepackRequired}
# Force clients to use texture packs in the current world
# Allowed values: "true" or "false"

content-log-file-enabled=${contentLogFileEnabled}
# Enables logging content errors to a file
# Allowed values: "true" or "false"

compression-threshold=${compressionThreshold}
# Determines the smallest size of raw network payload to compress
# Allowed values: 0-65535

server-authoritative-movement=${serverAuthoritativeMovement}
# Allowed values: "client-auth", "server-auth", "server-auth-with-rewind"
# Enables server authoritative movement. If "server-auth", the server will replay local user input on
# the server and send down corrections when the client's position doesn't match the server's.
# If "server-auth-with-rewind" is enabled and the server sends a correction, the clients will be instructed
# to rewind time back to the correction time, apply the correction, then replay all the player's inputs since then. This results in smoother and more frequent corrections.
# Corrections will only happen if correct-player-movement is set to true.

player-movement-score-threshold=${playerMovementScoreThreshold}
# The number of incongruent time intervals needed before abnormal behavior is reported.
# Disabled by server-authoritative-movement.

player-movement-distance-threshold=${playerMovementDistanceThreshold}
# The difference between server and client positions that needs to be exceeded before abnormal behavior is detected.
# Disabled by server-authoritative-movement.

player-movement-duration-threshold-in-ms=${playerMovementDurationThresholdInMs}
# The duration of time the server and client positions can be out of sync (as defined by player-movement-distance-threshold)
# before the abnormal movement score is incremented. This value is defined in milliseconds.
# Disabled by server-authoritative-movement.

correct-player-movement=${correctPlayerMovement}
# If true, the client position will get corrected to the server position if the movement score exceeds the threshold.


server-authoritative-block-breaking=${serverAuthoritativeBlockBreaking}
# If true, the server will compute block mining operations in sync with the client so it can verify that the client should be able to break blocks when it thinks it can.

script.name: Download latest release
Player Query
backup server properties
script.code:
#!/bin/bash

# Set the target directory (modify this path as needed)
target_dir="${ThisService_RootDirectory:-/path/to/default/directory}"
targetFile="$target_dir/server.properties"
backupFile="$target_dir/server.properties.backup"

# Check if target directory exists
if [ ! -d "$target_dir" ]; then
    echo "Error: Directory $target_dir does not exist."
    exit 1
fi

if [ -f "$targetFile" ]; then
    mv "$targetFile" "$backupFile"
    echo "Renamed $targetFile to $backupFile"
else
    echo "$targetFile not found in the specified directory."
fi

script.name: restore server properties
script.code:
#!/bin/bash

# Set the target directory and file paths
target_dir="${ThisService_RootDirectory}"
targetFile="$target_dir/server.properties"
backupFile="$target_dir/server.properties.backup"

if [ -f "$backupFile" ]; then
    if [ -f "$targetFile" ]; then
        rm "$targetFile"
        echo "Deleted existing $targetFile"
    fi
    mv "$backupFile" "$targetFile"
    echo "Renamed $backupFile to server.properties"
else
    echo "$backupFile not found in the specified directory."
fi
